title: hedyotis bartlingii
intro: Hedyotis bartlingii, also known as Indian snakeroot or bartling's hediotis, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. Its leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine to aid in the reduction of fever, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and provide relief from menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. Hedyotis bartlingii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
